message:
feat(frankenterm/main): tick 225 notification pipeline event loop cx-first (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Second cross-crate caller migration following tick 224. The
notification pipeline event-loop spawn at main.rs L14956 now
drives cx-first entry points for both the detection-read and
the detection-dispatch:

- `subscriber.recv_cx(&cx)` instead of `subscriber.recv()`
  (events.rs L736 — observes cx via broadcast_recv_with_cx)
- `pipeline.handle_detection_with_cx(&cx, ...)` instead of
  `pipeline.handle_detection(...)` (notifications.rs L381 —
  routes mute-check through is_event_muted_with_cx per tick 198
  AND dispatches through send_with_cx on each NotificationSender
  impl per tick 211/212 overrides)
- Top-of-loop `cx.checkpoint()` breaks the event loop cleanly
  on cx-cancel

Uses the same `Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(for_request)`
pattern as tick 224's workflow runner spawn — picks up ambient
cx from the runtime driver if present, otherwise creates a
fresh request-scoped cx.

Effect: a cx-cancel on the outer runtime now cleanly aborts
BOTH the workflow runner (tick 224) AND the notification
pipeline event loops. Previously each silently dropped cx
context at its spawn boundary.

Verification: `cargo check -p frankenterm --bin ft` compiles
clean via fork bypass (CC=llvm clang,
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
